prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, gather 'round, ye landlubbers, and I'll tell ye why the firmament above be painted such a brilliant azure! 'Tis the work o' the sun's rays, dancin' through the very air like me ship cuttin' through waves. When them golden beams come tumblin' down from the heavens, they scatter like me crew after a rum-soaked night! The shorter waves o' blue light, they be nimble devils, bouncin' about more than the longer waves. So when ye look up, ye see nothin' but that brilliant blue, stretchin' from horizon to horizon like the most perfect sail ye ever did witness! The good Lord himself must've mixed that color special-like, to give us mariners a sight to warm our hearts when the sea turns dark and treacherous. Blue as me finest naval coat, blue as the deepest waters we sail - that be why the sky shines so, me hearties! *takes a swig from a rum bottle*
